title: Research lead
description: Auditable output contract for one lead emitted by research-lead-finder. A lead is a specific, checkable connection or tension between retrieved sources that bears on the goal. It is not a topic, a summary, or a restatement that two papers share an area.

id.description: Stable identifier: sha1 of the normalized claim text. Used for dedup against the archive. record_lead.py computes this if absent.

goal_ref.description: Short slug of the goal this lead serves, from references/goal.md.

claim.description: The lead itself. A specific connection or tension that bears on the goal and could be checked. Not a topic and not a summary.

join_rationale.description: Which retrieved items were joined and why this specific connection follows. The combinatorial step made explicit.

arxiv_id.description: arXiv identifier of a paper returned by retrieval this run.

supporting_quote.description: Verbatim span from the abstract (or noted full text) that supports this lead's use of the source. Grounding must be point-to-able. A paraphrase is not a supporting quote.

support_type.description: What the supporting_quotes are drawn from. 'inferred' means no direct textual support was found: a disclosed weakness, not a pass.

assessed.description: True only if every source's supporting_quote was checked against the source text this run.

note.description: Honest grounding note. State partial or inferred support plainly. Flag if full-text verification is warranted but was not done.

novelty_note.description: Honest novelty statement. At MVP this can only say 'not found in current archive / searched set'. Never 'unexplored' or 'novel to the field'.

status.description: MVP emits 'candidate'. v1 tournament and QD ranking extend this enum.

emitted_by.description: Skill name and version, e.g. research-lead-finder@0.1.0. Provenance.
